drills.title: Failed Worker Verification
signals: worker run result has pass=false
verifyResults include a non-zero exit code
plan result synthesis is failed or mixed
operatorSteps: Inspect the run with `bun run samantha runs:show <run-id>` and the work chain with `bun run samantha review:show <run-id> --subject=run`.
Use `/recover` or a local recovery request so the new task is planned from the project profile canonical repoRoot.
Keep the failed worker worktree as evidence only; do not dispatch recovery from it.
Record the drill outcome with `bun run samantha drills:record failed-verify --outcome=<fixed|still_blocked|needs_bk> --note=<summary>`.

recoveryGuidance.canonicalRoot: Use the canonical repoRoot from `references/project-profiles/samantha.json` after profile expansion.
preMerge: Do not merge a run whose verification failed.
Create a recovery plan from the canonical project profile root.
Run the recovery task through normal verification before any merge gate.
postMerge: If a bad change was already merged, propose an explicit revert or forward-fix task from the canonical project profile root.
Do not use git reset or checkout to rewrite history.
Run verification, merge, push, and cleanup gates normally.
rollbackAuthority: Rollback is allowed only through deterministic recovery planning or explicit operator action.
Workers, non-writers, and orchestrator agents must not roll back state directly.

drills.title: Dirty Worktree Before Integration
signals: merge:check reports uncommitted changes
run lifecycle is missing mergedAt
operator review reports partial integration
operatorSteps: Inspect the run and lifecycle with `bun run samantha review:show <run-id> --subject=run`.
Leave unrelated user changes untouched and identify whether the dirt belongs to the run under review.
If the run is invalid, request a recovery task from the canonical project profile root rather than cleaning the old worker worktree by hand.
Record the drill outcome with `bun run samantha drills:record dirty-worktree --outcome=<fixed|still_blocked|needs_bk> --note=<summary>`.

recoveryGuidance.canonicalRoot: Use the canonical repoRoot from the selected project profile; worker worktree paths are evidence only.
preMerge: Do not apply merge while the integration target is dirty.
Resolve ownership of dirty files through a new approved task or BK decision.
Re-run merge:check after the canonical root is clean.
postMerge: If dirt is discovered after merge but before push, stop at the push gate and review the lifecycle record.
If pushed already, create an explicit follow-up recovery plan instead of rewriting history.
Cleanup remains gated and must not remove evidence before review.
rollbackAuthority: Rollback is allowed only through deterministic recovery planning, BK decision, or explicit operator action.
Workers, non-writers, and orchestrator agents must not clean, reset, or roll back dirty state directly.

drills.title: Merge Conflict
signals: merge:apply exits non-zero
merge gate reports non-fast-forward or conflicting changes
run lifecycle is not marked merged
operatorSteps: Inspect `bun run samantha merge:check --run-log=<path> --repo-root=<canonical-root>` output and the review report.
Treat the conflict as a blocked merge, not an invitation to force merge.
Create a recovery or rebase task from the canonical project profile root with the conflict evidence attached.
Record the drill outcome with `bun run samantha drills:record merge-conflict --outcome=<fixed|still_blocked|needs_bk> --note=<summary>`.

recoveryGuidance.canonicalRoot: Use the canonical project profile repoRoot selected for the original plan.
preMerge: Do not bypass merge:check or merge:apply.
Do not edit the worker worktree as the recovery root.
Run verification again after the conflict is resolved by a new approved task.
postMerge: If an incorrect conflict resolution was merged, create an explicit corrective task.
Do not rewrite published history from Samantha.
Push and cleanup remain separate gated transitions.
rollbackAuthority: Rollback is allowed only through a governed corrective task or explicit operator action.
Workers, non-writers, and orchestrator agents must not force merge, reset, or roll back state directly.

drills.title: Failed Push
signals: merge lifecycle has mergedAt but no pushedAt
merge:push exits non-zero
operator review reports partial integration
operatorSteps: Inspect `bun run samantha review:show <run-id> --subject=run` and the push command failure.
Do not mark pushedAt manually and do not cleanup the worktree before push status is settled.
If branch state changed remotely, request BK guidance or a recovery task from the canonical project profile root.
Record the drill outcome with `bun run samantha drills:record failed-push --outcome=<fixed|still_blocked|needs_bk> --note=<summary>`.

recoveryGuidance.canonicalRoot: Use the canonical project profile repoRoot; the merged commit and run log are audit evidence.
preMerge: Not applicable after mergedAt exists; verify the lifecycle before retrying a push gate.
If no merge occurred, resolve through the merge gate first.
Do not skip directly to cleanup.
postMerge: Keep the lifecycle in partially integrated state until push succeeds or BK chooses a recovery path.
Retrying push requires an explicit operator action, not automatic worker retry.
Cleanup requires the normal cleanup gate after push status is recorded.
rollbackAuthority: Rollback is allowed only through BK/operator direction or a new deterministic recovery plan.
Workers, non-writers, and orchestrator agents must not mark push state, retry, or roll back directly.

drills.title: Stale Approval
signals: decision subject no longer points to a current planned plan
plan is materialized, canceled, superseded, or missing
`/go` reports no actionable current approval
operatorSteps: Inspect pending decisions with `bun run samantha decisions:list --pending` and current state with `bun run samantha ceo:status`.
Reject, archive, or revise stale decisions through normal decision commands.
Create a fresh plan approval if the work is still desired; do not reuse stale approval evidence.
Record the drill outcome with `bun run samantha drills:record stale-approval --outcome=<fixed|still_blocked|needs_bk> --note=<summary>`.

recoveryGuidance.canonicalRoot: A fresh plan must use the selected canonical project profile repoRoot.
preMerge: Do not materialize tasks from stale approval.
Generate a new plan or revision and wait for current BK approval.
Keep stale decision records as audit history.
postMerge: If stale approval was discovered after integration, use review:show to reconstruct the chain.
Create a corrective plan if the integrated work was not actually approved.
Do not edit old decisions to make them current.
rollbackAuthority: Rollback is allowed only through current decision commands, deterministic recovery planning, or explicit operator action.
Workers, non-writers, and orchestrator agents must not reuse stale approvals or roll back state directly.

drills.title: Mistaken Profile Proposal
signals: profile proposal grants writer authority to a non-writer
profile proposal changes model or worktree policy without approval
profile governance reports unapproved authority change
operatorSteps: Inspect the profile/capability decision and governance violations before approval.
Reject the mistaken proposal or request a revised report-only profile change.
Do not activate profile changes from LLM output alone.
Record the drill outcome with `bun run samantha drills:record mistaken-profile-proposal --outcome=<fixed|still_blocked|needs_bk> --note=<summary>`.

recoveryGuidance.canonicalRoot: Profile proposals are reviewed against canonical repository profiles and policy records, not worker worktrees.
preMerge: Keep `codex-worker` as the only production writer unless a later evidence phase explicitly changes policy.
Require a governed `agent_profile_change` approval for activation.
Reject proposals that silently expand worktree or merge authority.
postMerge: If a mistaken profile file was merged, create a corrective profile-governance task from the canonical project profile root.
Record the original mistake and correction as governance events.
Run verification before merge, push, and cleanup.
rollbackAuthority: Rollback is allowed only through governed profile correction or explicit operator action.
Workers, non-writers, and orchestrator agents must not activate, revert, or roll back profile authority directly.

drills.title: Blocked Capability Request
signals: connector or secret access is requested without an approved capability record
skill bundle conflicts with Samantha orchestration safety policy
capability governance reports missing or blocked grants
operatorSteps: Inspect the capability decision and denial report without exposing secret values.
Reject blocked requests or create a narrower governed capability proposal.
Do not pass connectors or secrets to workers unless an approved capability record grants that exact access.
Record the drill outcome with `bun run samantha drills:record blocked-capability-request --outcome=<fixed|still_blocked|needs_bk> --note=<summary>`.

recoveryGuidance.canonicalRoot: Capability corrections must be proposed against project and agent profile records from the canonical repoRoot.
preMerge: Do not dispatch work that depends on missing connector or secret grants.
Keep denial reports redacted and audit-friendly.
Require a separate governed capability approval for allowed skill bundles, connectors, and secrets.
postMerge: If an unsafe grant was merged, create a corrective governance task and revoke the grant through normal approval gates.
Do not hide the unsafe proposal by deleting historical records.
Run profile governance tests before integration gates.
rollbackAuthority: Rollback is allowed only through governed capability correction or explicit operator action.
Workers, non-writers, and orchestrator agents must not grant, revoke, or roll back connector or secret authority directly.
